name: Alcippeidae
description: Alcippeidae is a family of small, active passerine birds found in forests across East and Southeast Asia. Formerly placed with the babblers, they are now recognized as a distinct family of fulvettas.

children.name: Alcippe
children.description: A genus of small, plain-faced birds with brown or grey plumage, found in Asian forests and scrub.

children.name: Alcippe poioicephala
children.description: The Brown-cheeked Fulvetta is a small, brownish bird with a grey crown and brown cheek patch. It forages actively in the understory of forests across South and Southeast Asia.

children.name: Alcippe morrisonia
children.description: The Grey-cheeked Fulvetta has a grey head, olive-brown upperparts, and pale buff underparts with a grey cheek. It inhabits montane forests from the Himalayas to Taiwan and Indochina.

children.name: Alcippe nipalensis
children.description: The Nepal Fulvetta is a small, brownish fulvetta with a warm buff breast and a greyish crown. It is found in hill forests of the Himalayas, foraging in mixed-species flocks.

children.name: Alcippe davidi
children.description: David's Fulvetta is a small, olive-brown bird with a rufous crown and pale underparts. It inhabits forests and scrub in southern China and northern mainland Southeast Asia.
